phases.goal: Understand your relationship patterns and set clear intentions

steps.title: Map your current relationships
steps.description: List the 10 most important people in your life. Rate each relationship 1–10 for depth and quality. Identify 2–3 you want to deepen and any you've been neglecting. Most people are surprised how small their meaningful circle actually is.
steps.success_metric: Relationship map completed and priorities identified

steps.title: Identify your blocking patterns
steps.description: Reflect honestly: Do you withdraw when stressed? Cancel plans too often? Struggle to be vulnerable? Keep conversation surface-level? The same pattern that limits one relationship limits all of them. Name it.
steps.success_metric: Top 1–2 blocking patterns identified in writing

steps.title: Commit to one new weekly social action
steps.description: Choose one concrete weekly action: send one voice note to a friend, arrange one coffee, attend one social event. Small consistent actions compound into a dramatically richer social life over 12 weeks.
steps.success_metric: Weekly social action completed for 2 consecutive weeks

phases.goal: Build deeper existing relationships and expand your social circle

steps.title: Practise deeper conversations
steps.description: Move beyond status updates. Ask: 'What's been the hardest part of your year?' 'What are you most excited about right now?' 'What are you figuring out?' Depth requires the first person to go first — that's usually you.
steps.success_metric: One genuinely deep conversation per week for 6 weeks

steps.title: Become a better listener
steps.description: For one month, focus exclusively on listening in conversations — not thinking about what to say next. Ask one follow-up question before contributing your own experience. The best social skill is also the simplest: genuine interest.
steps.success_metric: Active listening consciously practised in every conversation for 4 weeks

steps.title: Join one community or recurring group
steps.description: The best friendships form through repeated unplanned interactions. A weekly sports team, a book club, a climbing gym, a volunteering group. Proximity and repetition build connection faster than one-off events.
steps.success_metric: Recurring group joined and attended for at least 6 weeks

phases.goal: Build a relationship maintenance system that doesn't rely on memory

steps.title: Create a simple relationship CRM
steps.description: Use a spreadsheet or notes app to track the people you want to stay in touch with and when you last reached out. Set reminders for birthdays, follow-ups, and check-ins. The people who feel most cared for are often maintained through simple systems.
steps.success_metric: Simple relationship tracker set up with 10+ key contacts

steps.title: Host or organise one social gathering
steps.description: Invite 4–8 people to something you organise — dinner, a hike, a board game night. The person who creates the container for connection builds a magnetic social life. You don't need to be the most outgoing person — just the one who shows up.
steps.success_metric: One social gathering hosted or organised
